etymology.description: 精(精) (jīng, "refined; essence") + 製(製) (zhì, "make; manufacture") + 品(品) (pǐn, "product; goods") refers to high-quality or carefully produced products. 精(精) (jīng, "essence") emphasizes the quality or selectiveness involved; 製(製) (zhì, "make") refers to the process of creation, highlighting manufacturing; 品(品) (pǐn, "product") signifies the outcome or goods.
etymology.interpretation: 精(精) (jīng, "refined; essence") suggests purity or selectiveness, leading to the idea of refinement in quality. 製(製) (zhì, "make; manufacture") refers to the act of creating or producing something, thus when associated with 精(精) (jīng, "refined"), it implies a process of creating something with care or thoroughness. 品(品) (pǐn, "product") is the result or output, giving the sense of carefully crafted or high-grade goods.

examples.chinese: 许多商店都出售各种精制品(許多商店都出售各種精製品)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō shāngdiàn dōu chūshòu gèzhǒng jīngzhìpǐn.
examples.english: Many stores sell various refined products.

examples.chinese: 这些精制品在市场上很受欢迎(這些精製品在市場上很受歡迎)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèxiē jīngzhìpǐn zài shìchǎng shàng hěn shòu huānyíng.
examples.english: These refined products are very popular in the market.

examples.chinese: 顾客对精制品的品质很挑剔(顧客對精製品的品質很挑剔)。
examples.pinyin: Gùkè duì jīngzhìpǐn de pǐnzhì hěn tiāotì.
examples.english: Customers are very picky about the quality of refined products.
